title: First stone tools
desc: Early human ancestors in Africa began chipping stones into sharp tools for cutting and pounding.

title: Oldowan tool culture
desc: Early humans across Africa made simple chopper tools by knocking flakes off pebbles.

title: Homo erectus appears
desc: A tall, upright human ancestor appeared in Africa and later spread across much of Asia.

title: Humans reach Asia
desc: Homo erectus traveled out of Africa and lived in places like the country of Georgia and China.

title: Acheulean hand axes
desc: Early humans learned to shape teardrop-shaped stone hand axes used for many tasks.

title: Early control of fire
desc: Some early humans began using fire for warmth, cooking, and protection.

title: Heidelberg humans
desc: A strong human ancestor lived in Africa and Europe and hunted large animals with spears.

title: Wooden hunting spears
desc: People in Germany carved wooden throwing spears to hunt horses and other animals.

title: Regular use of fire
desc: By this time many groups of early humans used hearths and fire every day.

title: Neanderthals emerge
desc: Neanderthals, a tough cousin of modern humans, lived across Europe and western Asia.

title: Homo sapiens emerge
desc: Modern humans first appear in Africa, distinct from earlier hominid species.

title: First modern humans
desc: The earliest known Homo sapiens lived in Morocco, in northern Africa.

title: Hafted stone tools
desc: People began attaching sharp stone points to wooden handles to make better tools and spears.

title: Middle Stone Age begins
desc: Humans in Africa made smaller, more refined stone tools and points for hunting.

title: Early sapiens in Greece
desc: A skull from a cave in Greece shows modern humans reached Europe very early.

title: Bhimbetka rock shelters
desc: Early humans lived in natural rock shelters in central India that were used for ages.

title: Modern humans in Africa
desc: Early Homo sapiens were living and spreading across the continent of Africa.

title: Neanderthal cave rings
desc: Neanderthals built mysterious rings out of stalagmites deep inside a French cave.

title: Use of red ochre
desc: People in Africa collected and ground red ochre, a colorful earth used for marking and decoration.

title: Early shellfish eating
desc: Humans living on the South African coast gathered and ate shellfish from the sea.

title: Neanderthal jewelry
desc: Neanderthals collected eagle talons and shells, possibly to wear as ornaments.

title: Modern humans in Arabia
desc: Homo sapiens spread out of Africa and left a fingerlike fossil in the Arabian desert.

title: Skhul and Qafzeh burials
desc: Modern humans were buried with care in caves in the land of present-day Israel.

title: Ochre paint workshop
desc: People at Blombos Cave in South Africa mixed and stored paint made from ochre in shells.

title: Earliest shell beads
desc: Humans pierced sea shells to wear as some of the first known jewelry.

title: Engraved ochre patterns
desc: People at Blombos Cave scratched crisscross patterns into pieces of ochre.

title: Toba super-eruption
desc: A giant volcano in Indonesia erupted, cooling the climate and challenging early humans.

title: Humans reach Australia
desc: People crossed the sea to Australia, becoming the ancestors of Aboriginal Australians.

title: Bow and arrow in Africa
desc: People at Sibudu Cave in South Africa may have used some of the first bows and arrows.

title: Out of Africa migration
desc: A big wave of modern humans left Africa and spread across Asia and beyond.

title: Madjedbebe rock shelter
desc: Early Australians left stone tools and ground ochre at this ancient shelter.

title: First sewing needles
desc: Humans began making bone needles to sew warm, fitted clothing from animal skins.

title: Oldest cave painting
desc: A painting of a wild pig in Sulawesi, Indonesia, is the oldest known animal art.

title: Humans reach Europe
desc: Modern humans moved into Europe, where Neanderthals already lived.

title: Sulawesi hunting scene
desc: A cave painting in Indonesia shows tiny human-like hunters chasing wild animals.

title: Early cave hand stencils
desc: People in Indonesia blew paint around their hands to leave stencils on cave walls.

title: Mammoth ivory flute
desc: People in Germany carved flutes from bird bone and mammoth ivory, making early music.

title: Lion Man figurine
desc: Someone in Germany carved a tall figure with a human body and a lion's head from ivory.

title: Neanderthals die out
desc: The last Neanderthals disappeared, leaving modern humans as the only humans on Earth.

title: Venus of Hohle Fels
desc: People in Germany carved one of the oldest known figures of a human, from mammoth ivory.

title: Aurignacian culture
desc: Early modern humans in Europe made fine blade tools, art, and ornaments.

title: Chauvet Cave paintings
desc: Artists in France painted lions, rhinos, and horses deep inside Chauvet Cave.

title: Oldest tally marks
desc: People carved notches into bones, perhaps to count days or keep records.

title: Sungir rich burials
desc: People in Russia buried their dead with thousands of carved ivory beads.

title: Domestication of dogs
desc: Wolves began living alongside humans and slowly became the first tame dogs.

title: Pavlov hunter camp
desc: People in the Czech lands built camps and made fired clay figures while hunting mammoths.

title: Oldest pottery objects
desc: Hunters in the Czech Republic shaped wet clay into animal figures and baked them hard.

title: Gravettian culture
desc: Hunters across Europe made small carved figures and used nets and traps.

title: Venus of Willendorf
desc: Someone in Austria carved a small, round stone figure of a woman.

title: Cosquer Cave art
desc: Artists painted animals in a French cave whose entrance is now under the sea.

title: Mammoth bone huts
desc: People on the cold plains of Ukraine built shelters from huge mammoth bones.

title: Coldest Ice Age peak
desc: Earth reached the coldest point of the last Ice Age, with huge ice sheets covering land.

title: Ohalo II lakeside camp
desc: People by a lake in Israel gathered wild grains and lived in brush huts.

title: Spear-thrower invented
desc: Hunters used a wooden lever called an atlatl to throw spears farther and harder.

title: Oldest known pottery
desc: People in southern China made some of the world's first clay pots for cooking.

title: Lascaux Cave paintings
desc: Artists in France covered cave walls with bulls, horses, and deer in glowing colors.

title: Magdalenian culture
desc: Skilled European hunters carved bone and antler tools and made beautiful cave art.

title: Altamira bison paintings
desc: Artists in Spain painted lifelike bison on the ceiling of Altamira Cave.

title: Humans reach the Americas
desc: People crossed from Asia into Alaska and began spreading through the Americas.

title: Cave paintings at Lascaux
desc: Artists in southern France cover cave walls with vivid animals and symbols.

title: Bison Licking Insect carving
desc: A hunter carved a graceful bison turning its head onto a spear-thrower in France.

title: Monte Verde settlement
desc: People lived in southern Chile, showing humans reached South America very early.

title: Jomon pottery culture
desc: People in Japan made decorated clay pots, among the oldest pottery traditions.

title: Clovis hunters in America
desc: People across North America made distinctive spear points to hunt big animals.

title: Younger Dryas cold snap
desc: Earth suddenly grew cold again for over a thousand years before warming for good.

title: Natufian villages
desc: People in the Middle East built some of the first permanent homes before farming began.

title: End of the Ice Age
desc: The last Ice Age ended as the climate warmed and great ice sheets melted away.

title: Mammoths near extinction
desc: Woolly mammoths and other giant Ice Age animals died out across most of the world.

title: Last Ice Age ends
desc: Glaciers retreat across the Northern Hemisphere, reshaping coastlines and enabling new migrations.

title: First wheat farming
desc: People in the Middle East began planting and harvesting wild wheat on purpose.

title: Agriculture begins
desc: Humans in the Fertile Crescent begin deliberately cultivating wheat and barley.

title: Gobekli Tepe built
desc: People in Turkey raised huge carved stone pillars to make the oldest known temple.

title: Jericho first settled
desc: One of the world's oldest towns grew up around a spring in the Jordan Valley.

title: Goats and sheep tamed
desc: People in the Middle East began herding goats and sheep for meat, milk, and wool.

title: Jericho founded
desc: One of the earliest known permanently settled towns takes shape in the Jordan Valley.

title: Domestication of cattle
desc: Farmers tamed wild cattle, gaining a source of meat, milk, and muscle power.

title: Walls of Jericho
desc: The town of Jericho built a great stone wall and tower around itself.

title: Pigs domesticated
desc: People in the Middle East and China tamed wild boar to raise as pigs.

title: Rice farming in China
desc: People along China's rivers began growing rice in wet fields.

title: Farming reaches everywhere
desc: The Neolithic Revolution spread as more people grew crops and kept animals.

title: Squash domesticated
desc: Early farmers in the Americas began growing squash, one of the first New World crops.

title: Potatoes in the Andes
desc: People high in the Andes Mountains of South America began growing potatoes.

title: Çatalhöyük thrives
desc: A densely populated Neolithic settlement in Anatolia houses thousands without streets or public squares.

title: Cats join human homes
desc: Wild cats started living near farming villages to hunt mice, slowly becoming pets.

title: Catalhoyuk town
desc: Thousands of people in Turkey lived in mud-brick houses entered through the roof.

title: Pottery invented
desc: Fired clay vessels appear independently in multiple regions, transforming food storage and cooking.

title: First woven cloth
desc: People learned to spin plant fibers into thread and weave them into linen cloth.

title: Mehrgarh village
desc: Farmers in present-day Pakistan grew grain and raised animals in an early village.

title: Maize farming begins
desc: People in Mexico began turning a wild grass called teosinte into corn.

title: Pottery spreads widely
desc: Farming villages across the world made clay pots to store and cook food.

title: Earliest copper use
desc: People in the Middle East hammered natural copper into beads and small tools.

title: Bananas grown in Guinea
desc: People in Papua New Guinea began growing bananas and other crops at Kuk Swamp.

title: Flax grown for linen
desc: Farmers grew flax plants to make oil and to weave into linen cloth.

title: First copper smelting
desc: People learned to melt copper out of rock, beginning the age of metals.

title: Irrigation farming
desc: Farmers in Mesopotamia dug channels to bring river water to their crops.

title: Sailing boats appear
desc: People in the Middle East began using sails to travel across water by wind.

title: Wine making begins
desc: People in the Caucasus region began crushing grapes to make wine.

title: First city of Eridu
desc: People in Mesopotamia built Eridu, one of the world's earliest cities.

title: Copper smelting begins
desc: Metalworkers in the Near East learn to smelt copper, opening the Chalcolithic Age.

title: Banpo village in China
desc: Farmers along the Yellow River lived in a village of round houses and grew millet.

title: Cotton grown for cloth
desc: People in South Asia and the Americas began growing cotton to make soft cloth.

title: Plows pulled by animals
desc: Farmers hitched oxen to wooden plows to turn the soil faster.

title: Megalithic tombs in Europe
desc: People built giant stone tombs called dolmens across western Europe.

title: Beer brewing begins
desc: Early farmers in the Middle East learned to brew beer from grain.

title: Pottery in the Americas
desc: People in South America began making some of the earliest clay pots in the New World.

title: Llamas domesticated
desc: People in the Andes tamed llamas to carry loads and provide wool.

title: Copper Age in Europe
desc: People across Europe began making tools and ornaments out of copper.

title: Varna gold treasure
desc: People in Bulgaria were buried with the oldest known worked gold objects.

title: Carnac standing stones
desc: People in France set up thousands of standing stones in long rows.

title: Horses domesticated
desc: People on the grasslands of Central Asia began taming wild horses.

title: Donkeys domesticated
desc: People in northeast Africa tamed wild donkeys to carry heavy loads.

title: Chickens domesticated
desc: People in Southeast Asia tamed wild jungle fowl, the ancestors of chickens.

title: Sahara turns to desert
desc: The once-green Sahara dried out, pushing people toward the Nile River valley.

title: Sorghum and millet farming
desc: People in Africa began growing hardy grains like sorghum and millet.

title: Olives grown for oil
desc: People around the Mediterranean began growing olive trees for their oil.

title: The wheel invented
desc: People in Mesopotamia or Europe invented the wheel for carts and pottery.

title: Silk discovered in China
desc: People in China learned to unwind silkworm cocoons to make fine silk thread.

title: Wheel invented
desc: The potter's wheel and wheeled vehicles appear in Mesopotamia and the Eurasian steppe.

title: Sumerian cities grow
desc: Large cities with temples rose across southern Mesopotamia.

title: Caral region settled
desc: People along the coast of Peru began building the first towns in the Americas.

title: Bronze Age begins
desc: The combination of copper and tin to make bronze spreads across Eurasia, transforming tools and weapons.

title: Otzi the Iceman
desc: A man crossing the Alps died and was frozen in ice with his copper axe for ages.

title: Bronze first made
desc: Metalworkers mixed copper and tin to make bronze, a harder and better metal.

title: Writing invented in Mesopotamia
desc: Sumerian scribes develop cuneiform, the earliest known writing system, to record trade and administration.

title: Newgrange tomb built
desc: People in Ireland built a huge stone tomb where sunlight enters on the shortest day.

title: First writing appears
desc: People in Mesopotamia pressed marks into clay to keep records, inventing writing.

title: Sumerian Cuneiform Writing
desc: People in Sumer began pressing wedge-shaped marks into clay to record words and numbers.

title: Egypt unified
desc: Upper and Lower Egypt are brought together under a single pharaoh, founding the First Dynasty.

title: Unification of Egypt
desc: King Narmer joined Upper and Lower Egypt into one kingdom ruled by a single pharaoh.

title: City of Uruk Flourishes
desc: Uruk in Mesopotamia grew into one of the world's first big cities with thousands of people.

title: Egyptian Hieroglyphs Develop
desc: Ancient Egyptians wrote with picture symbols called hieroglyphs on stone and papyrus.

title: Sail Boats on the Nile
desc: Egyptians used boats with sails to travel and trade along the Nile River.

title: First Stonehenge phase
desc: People in England dug the first circular ditch and bank that became Stonehenge.

title: Early Bronze Working
desc: Metalworkers learned to mix copper and tin to make hard, useful bronze tools and weapons.

title: Stonehenge Building Begins
desc: People in Britain started building Stonehenge, a giant circle of standing stones.

title: Wheel Used for Transport
desc: People in Mesopotamia began using wheels on carts to carry heavy loads.

title: Indus Valley Towns Grow
desc: Villages along the Indus River grew into planned towns with brick houses and drains.

title: First Egyptian Dynasty
desc: Egypt's earliest line of pharaohs began ruling from the city of Memphis.

title: Plow Farming Spreads
desc: Farmers used animal-pulled plows to break up soil and grow more grain.

title: Gilgamesh Rules Uruk
desc: A king named Gilgamesh ruled Uruk and later became the hero of famous stories.

title: Cycladic Marble Figures
desc: Islanders in the Aegean Sea carved simple, smooth statues from white marble.

title: Pharaoh Djoser's Reign
desc: Djoser ruled Egypt and ordered a grand stone tomb built for himself.

title: Step Pyramid of Saqqara
desc: The architect Imhotep designed Egypt's first pyramid, built in giant stone steps.

title: Indus Valley civilization
desc: Sophisticated urban centers with planned streets and drainage systems flourish in the Indian subcontinent.

title: Sumerian City-States War
desc: Rival Sumerian cities like Ur and Lagash fought over land and water.

title: Royal Tombs of Ur
desc: Kings and queens of Ur were buried with gold jewelry, harps, and treasures.

title: Caral City in Peru
desc: People in Peru built Caral, one of the oldest cities in the Americas, with stone pyramids.

title: Great Pyramid built
desc: Pharaoh Khufu's tomb rises at Giza as one of the largest structures ever built by human hands.

title: Great Pyramid of Giza
desc: Pharaoh Khufu had the largest pyramid in Egypt built as his tomb.

title: Khafre's Pyramid Built
desc: Pharaoh Khafre built the second great pyramid at Giza near his father's.

title: Stonehenge constructed
desc: Massive standing stones are arranged on Salisbury Plain in a pattern aligned with the solstices.

title: The Great Sphinx Carved
desc: Egyptians carved a giant lion with a human head from solid rock near the pyramids.

title: Mohenjo-daro Built
desc: The Indus people built Mohenjo-daro, a city with straight streets and a great public bath.

title: Harappa Trading City
desc: Harappa grew into a busy Indus Valley city that traded goods far and wide.

title: Standardized Weights in India
desc: Indus Valley traders used carefully made stone cubes as standard weights.

title: Sumerian Mathematics
desc: Sumerians counted using a system based on the number sixty, which we still use for time.

title: Liangzhu Jade Culture
desc: People in eastern China carved beautiful jade objects and built large earthen mounds.

title: Lagash and Umma Border War
desc: Two Sumerian cities fought a long quarrel over a farming border.

title: Stele of the Vultures
desc: A king of Lagash carved a stone monument showing his army's victory in battle.

title: Pyramid Texts Written
desc: Egyptians carved spells inside royal tombs to help dead kings reach the afterlife.

title: Ebla Trading Kingdom
desc: The Syrian city of Ebla grew rich from trade and kept thousands of clay records.

title: Sargon Founds Akkad
desc: Sargon conquered the cities of Mesopotamia and built the world's first empire.

title: Akkadian Empire Spreads
desc: Sargon's empire stretched across Mesopotamia and used one common language for rule.

title: Enheduanna the Poet
desc: A princess named Enheduanna wrote hymns and became the first known author by name.

title: Naram-Sin's Victory Stele
desc: King Naram-Sin of Akkad raised a carved stone showing himself climbing a mountain in triumph.

title: Egyptian Old Kingdom Ends
desc: Egypt broke apart into rival regions as central royal power weakened.

title: Fall of the Akkadian Empire
desc: The Akkadian Empire collapsed after droughts and attacks from outside tribes.

title: First Intermediate Period
desc: Egypt went through a time of weak kings, hunger, and local rulers fighting.

title: Gudea Rules Lagash
desc: The peaceful ruler Gudea rebuilt temples in Lagash and left many statues of himself.

title: Third Dynasty of Ur
desc: Kings of Ur rebuilt a strong, organized kingdom across southern Mesopotamia.

title: Great Ziggurat of Ur
desc: King Ur-Nammu built a massive stepped temple tower for the moon god.

title: Code of Ur-Nammu
desc: King Ur-Nammu wrote one of the earliest known sets of written laws.

title: Epic of Gilgamesh Stories
desc: Storytellers spread tales of the hero Gilgamesh searching for everlasting life.

title: Xia Dynasty in China
desc: Chinese tradition says the Xia were China's first ruling dynasty along the Yellow River.

title: Middle Kingdom of Egypt
desc: Pharaoh Mentuhotep reunited Egypt and began a new age of strong rule.

title: Fall of Ur to Elam
desc: The city of Ur was destroyed when Elamite invaders attacked from the east.

title: Minoan Palaces on Crete
desc: The Minoans built large palaces with colorful wall paintings on the island of Crete.

title: Domestication of the Horse
desc: People on the grasslands began riding and harnessing horses for travel and work.

title: Stonehenge Sarsen Circle
desc: Builders raised the giant upright sarsen stones at Stonehenge into a ring.

title: Assyrian Trade Colonies
desc: Merchants from Assur set up trading posts in Anatolia to sell tin and cloth.

title: Abraham's Journey
desc: According to the Bible, Abraham left Mesopotamia and traveled to the land of Canaan.

title: Hittites Rise in Anatolia
desc: The Hittite people built a powerful kingdom in what is now Turkey.

title: Decline of Indus Cities
desc: The great Indus Valley cities slowly emptied as rivers and climate changed.

title: Mathematical Papyri in Egypt
desc: Egyptian scribes wrote problems about fractions and shapes on papyrus scrolls.

title: Babylon Becomes Powerful
desc: The city of Babylon grew from a small town into a leading Mesopotamian power.

title: Minoan Linear A Script
desc: The Minoans wrote with a script called Linear A that we still cannot fully read.

title: Reign of Hammurabi
desc: Hammurabi became king of Babylon and conquered much of Mesopotamia.

title: Hammurabi's Code
desc: The Babylonian king inscribes one of history's earliest comprehensive legal codes on a stone stele.

title: Code of Hammurabi
desc: King Hammurabi carved a famous list of laws on a tall black stone pillar.

title: Mycenaean Greeks Rise
desc: Warrior kings built strong stone fortresses across mainland Greece.

title: Shaft Graves at Mycenae
desc: Mycenaean kings were buried with golden masks and weapons in deep grave pits.

title: Hyksos Rule Northern Egypt
desc: Foreign rulers called the Hyksos took control of part of Egypt for a time.

title: Hittite King Hattusili I
desc: Hattusili I expanded the Hittite kingdom and made Hattusa its capital.

title: Thera Volcano Erupts
desc: A huge volcano on the island of Thera exploded and reshaped the Aegean world.

title: Shang Dynasty Begins
desc: The Shang kings ruled northern China and made fine bronze vessels.

title: Chariots Spread Widely
desc: Fast horse-drawn chariots became powerful weapons in armies across the Near East.

title: Hittites Sack Babylon
desc: A Hittite army raided far south and destroyed the great city of Babylon.

title: Kassites Rule Babylon
desc: The Kassite people took over Babylon and ruled it peacefully for centuries.

title: New Kingdom of Egypt
desc: Pharaoh Ahmose drove out the Hyksos and began Egypt's powerful New Kingdom.

title: Kingdom of Mitanni
desc: The Mitanni built a strong kingdom in northern Syria and trained chariot horses.

title: Olmec Civilization Begins
desc: The Olmec people of Mexico built towns and carved giant stone heads.

title: Queen Hatshepsut Reigns
desc: Hatshepsut ruled Egypt as a female pharaoh and built grand monuments.

title: Hatshepsut rules Egypt
desc: Egypt's most famous female pharaoh governs as king for over two decades, commissioning major building projects.

title: Thutmose III's Conquests
desc: Pharaoh Thutmose III led many campaigns and expanded Egypt's empire.

title: Battle of Megiddo
desc: Thutmose III won a major battle against Canaanite cities at Megiddo.

title: Mycenaean Linear B Script
desc: Greek scribes used Linear B writing to keep palace records on clay tablets.

title: Mycenaeans Take Crete
desc: Mainland Greeks took control of the Minoan palaces on Crete.

title: Amenhotep III's Golden Age
desc: Egypt grew rich and peaceful under Pharaoh Amenhotep III's long reign.

title: Akhenaten's New Religion
desc: Pharaoh Akhenaten told Egypt to worship only the sun disk called the Aten.

title: Assyria Grows Strong
desc: Assyria broke free from Mitanni and became an independent power again.

title: City of Amarna Built
desc: Akhenaten built a brand new capital city in the desert for his sun god.

title: Bust of Nefertiti Made
desc: An Egyptian sculptor carved a famous painted bust of Queen Nefertiti.

title: Hittite King Suppiluliuma
desc: Suppiluliuma I made the Hittite kingdom one of the strongest in the region.

title: Tutankhamun's Reign
desc: The boy king Tutankhamun restored Egypt's old gods before dying young.

title: Reign of Ramesses II
desc: Ramesses the Great ruled Egypt for many years and built huge temples.

title: Battle of Kadesh
desc: Pharaoh Ramesses II and the Hittites fought a giant chariot battle at Kadesh.

title: Abu Simbel Temples
desc: Ramesses II carved enormous statues of himself into a cliff at Abu Simbel.

title: Egypt-Hittite Peace Treaty
desc: Egypt and the Hittites signed one of the world's first known peace treaties.

title: Moses leads the Exodus
desc: According to the Hebrew scriptures, Moses leads the Israelites out of Egypt toward the Promised Land.

title: Oracle Bones in China
desc: Shang priests scratched questions onto bones and shells to ask about the future.

title: Merneptah Stele Mentions Israel
desc: An Egyptian victory monument made the earliest known mention of Israel.

title: Trojan War
desc: Greek city-states lay siege to Troy in a conflict later immortalized in Homer's Iliad.

title: Iron Age begins
desc: Iron-smelting technology spreads across the Old World, replacing bronze as the dominant metal.

title: Olmec civilization rises
desc: The Olmec build monumental earthworks and colossal stone heads along Mexico's Gulf Coast.

title: Sea Peoples Attack
desc: Mysterious raiders called the Sea Peoples struck cities around the Mediterranean.

title: Trojan War Tradition
desc: Greek legend tells of a long war fought against the city of Troy.

title: Iron Tools Spread
desc: People across the Near East began making stronger tools and weapons from iron.

title: Olmec City of San Lorenzo
desc: San Lorenzo became a major Olmec center with carved stone monuments.

title: Vedic Hymns in India
desc: Priests in India composed and chanted sacred Vedic hymns to their gods.

title: Fall of the Hittite Empire
desc: The mighty Hittite kingdom collapsed and its capital Hattusa was abandoned.

title: Mycenaean Palaces Burn
desc: The great Greek palace centers were destroyed and abandoned.

title: Bronze Age Collapse
desc: Many great kingdoms across the eastern Mediterranean suddenly fell apart.

title: Ramesses III Defeats Sea Peoples
desc: Pharaoh Ramesses III won great battles to keep the Sea Peoples out of Egypt.

title: Phoenician Cities Rise
desc: Sea-trading cities like Tyre and Sidon grew rich along the coast of Lebanon.

title: Greek Dark Age Begins
desc: Greece entered a quiet time when writing and big cities mostly disappeared.

title: Israelite Tribes in Canaan
desc: The tribes of Israel settled in the hills of Canaan and farmed the land.

title: Aramaeans Spread Across Syria
desc: Aramaean tribes settled across Syria and spread their language widely.

title: Phoenician Alphabet
desc: Phoenician traders used a simple alphabet that became the root of many others.

title: Zhou Dynasty Begins
desc: The Zhou overthrew the Shang and began China's longest-lasting dynasty.

title: Mandate of Heaven Idea
desc: The Zhou said heaven gave good kings the right to rule and could take it away.

title: King David Rules Israel
desc: David united the Israelite tribes and made Jerusalem his capital city.

title: Nubian Kingdom Grows
desc: The kingdom of Kush in Nubia grew strong along the southern Nile.

title: Painted Grey Ware Culture
desc: People in northern India made distinctive grey pottery during the early Iron Age.

title: Tyrian Purple Dye Trade
desc: Phoenicians made a rare purple dye from sea snails and sold it across the sea.

title: Reign of King Solomon
desc: Solomon ruled Israel in wealth and was remembered for his great wisdom.

title: King Solomon's Temple
desc: King Solomon built a grand temple in Jerusalem for the worship of God.

title: Kingdom of Israel Splits
desc: After Solomon, the Israelites divided into the kingdoms of Israel and Judah.

title: Neo-Assyrian Empire Begins
desc: Assyria started building a fierce new empire with a powerful army.

title: Olmec City of La Venta
desc: La Venta became the leading Olmec city with pyramids and giant stone heads.

title: Chavin Culture in Peru
desc: The Chavin people of Peru built temples and shared art across the Andes.

title: Ashurnasirpal II's Palace
desc: An Assyrian king built a grand palace at Nimrud decorated with stone carvings.

title: Urartu Kingdom in Armenia
desc: The mountain kingdom of Urartu built strong forts and skilled metalwork.

title: Carthage Founded
desc: Phoenician settlers founded the trading city of Carthage in North Africa.

title: Greek Alphabet Adopted
desc: The Greeks added vowels to the Phoenician alphabet to write their language.

title: Greek City-States Form
desc: Independent Greek cities called poleis began to grow with their own governments.

title: Adena Mound Builders
desc: Native peoples in North America began building large earthen burial mounds.

title: Hallstatt Iron Age Culture
desc: Early Celtic peoples in central Europe mined salt and worked iron.

title: Phoenician Glassmaking
desc: Phoenician craftsmen became famous for making and trading colorful glass.

title: First Olympic Games
desc: The Greeks held athletic games at Olympia to honor the god Zeus.

title: Spring and Autumn Period
desc: Zhou China split into many states that competed in a long age of rivalry.

title: Eastern Zhou Period
desc: The Zhou kings moved their capital east as their power over China weakened.

title: Rome founded
desc: According to Roman tradition, Romulus establishes the city on the banks of the Tiber River.

title: Founding of Rome
desc: Roman tradition says the city of Rome was founded by Romulus.

title: Homer composes the Iliad
desc: The epic poems attributed to Homer are set down, shaping Greek identity and literature for centuries.

title: Homer's Epics Composed
desc: Greek poets shaped the Iliad and Odyssey from old tales of heroes and war.

title: Greek Colonies Spread
desc: Greeks sailed out to found new colony cities around the Mediterranean and Black Sea.

title: Nubian Capital at Napata
desc: The kingdom of Kush ruled from the city of Napata near the Nile.

title: Tiglath-Pileser III Reforms Assyria
desc: This Assyrian king reorganized the army and greatly expanded the empire.

title: Syracuse Founded in Sicily
desc: Greek colonists from Corinth founded the powerful city of Syracuse.

title: Kushite Conquest of Egypt
desc: Kings from Kush in Nubia conquered Egypt and ruled as pharaohs.

title: Fall of the Kingdom of Israel
desc: Assyria conquered the northern kingdom of Israel and scattered its people.

title: Sargon II Builds a Capital
desc: Assyrian king Sargon II built a brand new capital city called Dur-Sharrukin.

title: Phrygian King Midas
desc: King Midas ruled Phrygia and became the subject of a tale about a golden touch.

title: Sennacherib Attacks Judah

title: Germany Wins the World Cup
desc: Germany won soccer's World Cup at the tournament held in Brazil.

title: Landing on a Comet
desc: Europe's Philae probe became the first spacecraft to land on a comet far out in space.

title: Ebola Outbreak in West Africa
desc: A serious outbreak of the Ebola virus in West Africa led to a major international health effort.

title: Malala Wins Nobel Prize
desc: Malala Yousafzai of Pakistan won the Nobel Peace Prize for defending girls' right to education.

title: Ice Bucket Challenge
desc: People worldwide poured ice water over themselves to raise money for research into a nerve disease.

title: Scotland Independence Vote
desc: People in Scotland voted on whether to leave the United Kingdom and chose to stay.

title: Same-sex marriage legalized in U.S.
desc: The Supreme Court rules in Obergefell v. Hodges that the Constitution guarantees same-sex couples the right to marry.

title: Paris Climate Agreement
desc: Nearly every country agreed in Paris to work together to limit global warming.

title: New Horizons Flies Past Pluto
desc: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft flew past Pluto and sent back the first close-up photos of the dwarf planet.

title: Same-Sex Marriage Nationwide
desc: The United States Supreme Court ruled that same-sex couples could marry in every state.

title: Global Goals Adopted
desc: The United Nations adopted seventeen Sustainable Development Goals to improve life around the world by 2030.

title: Star Wars Returns
desc: Star Wars: The Force Awakens brought the beloved space saga back to cinemas after many years.

title: Flowing Water Found on Mars
desc: Scientists announced signs that liquid water may sometimes flow on the surface of Mars.

title: Rio Summer Olympics
desc: Rio de Janeiro hosted the first Summer Olympic Games ever held in South America.

title: Brexit Referendum
desc: People in the United Kingdom voted to leave the European Union in a vote called Brexit.

title: Donald Trump Elected
desc: Businessman Donald Trump was elected president of the United States.

title: Gravitational Waves Detected
desc: Scientists detected gravitational waves, tiny ripples in space first predicted by Albert Einstein.

title: Computer Masters the Game of Go
desc: The AlphaGo program beat a top human champion at the ancient and complicated board game Go.

title: Pokemon Go Craze
desc: The phone game Pokemon Go sent millions of players walking outside to catch creatures using their cameras.

title: Juno Reaches Jupiter
desc: NASA's Juno spacecraft arrived at Jupiter to study the largest planet in our solar system.

title: Leicester City Win the League
desc: The underdog soccer club Leicester City stunned everyone by winning England's Premier League.

title: Total Eclipse Crosses America
desc: A total solar eclipse swept across the United States as the Moon blocked out the Sun.

title: Cassini Ends Its Mission
desc: After many years of discovery, the Cassini spacecraft dived into Saturn to finish its journey.

title: iPhone X and Face ID
desc: Apple released the iPhone X, which let users unlock the phone just by looking at it.

title: Hurricanes Strike the Caribbean
desc: Powerful hurricanes including Harvey, Irma, and Maria caused heavy damage in the Caribbean and the United States.

title: Despacito Tops the Charts
desc: The Spanish-language song Despacito became one of the most-watched videos in the world.

title: PyeongChang Winter Olympics
desc: South Korea hosted the Winter Olympic Games in the mountain town of PyeongChang.

title: France Wins the World Cup
desc: France won soccer's World Cup at the tournament held in Russia.

title: Harry and Meghan Marry
desc: Prince Harry married American actress Meghan Markle in a royal wedding in England.

title: Falcon Heavy Launches a Car
desc: SpaceX launched its powerful Falcon Heavy rocket, sending a red sports car into space as a test payload.

title: Thai Cave Rescue
desc: A youth soccer team and their coach were safely rescued after being trapped in a flooded cave in Thailand.

title: Black Panther Film
desc: The superhero movie Black Panther became a worldwide hit and a cultural milestone.

title: InSight Lands on Mars
desc: NASA's InSight lander touched down on Mars to study earthquakes deep inside the planet.

title: First Image of a Black Hole
desc: Scientists revealed the first-ever photograph of a black hole, captured by telescopes around the world.

title: Notre-Dame Cathedral Fire
desc: A fire badly damaged the famous Notre-Dame cathedral in Paris, sparking a worldwide effort to rebuild it.

title: Global Climate Strikes
desc: Millions of young people, inspired by activist Greta Thunberg, marched to demand action on climate change.

title: Apollo 11 Turns Fifty
desc: The world celebrated fifty years since astronauts first walked on the Moon.

title: US Women Win the World Cup
desc: The United States women's team won soccer's World Cup in France.

title: Avengers Endgame Record
desc: The superhero film Avengers: Endgame became one of the highest-earning movies ever made.

title: COVID-19 pandemic begins
desc: A novel coronavirus spreads from China across the globe, triggering the worst pandemic in a century.

title: Astronauts Fly on Crew Dragon
desc: SpaceX carried astronauts to the space station, the first crewed launch from the United States in years.

title: Perseverance Heads to Mars
desc: NASA launched the Perseverance rover and a tiny helicopter on a journey toward Mars.

title: Australian Bushfires
desc: Enormous bushfires burned across Australia, affecting people, wildlife, and forests.

title: United Kingdom Leaves the EU
desc: The United Kingdom officially left the European Union, completing the process known as Brexit.

title: Tokyo Olympics Postponed
desc: The Tokyo Summer Olympics were postponed for a year because of the COVID-19 pandemic.

title: Helicopter Flies on Mars
desc: NASA's tiny Ingenuity helicopter made the first powered flight on another planet.

title: James Webb Telescope Launched
desc: The powerful James Webb Space Telescope launched on a mission to see the earliest stars and galaxies.

title: Tokyo Olympics Held
desc: The delayed Summer Olympics finally took place in Tokyo, mostly without fans in the stands.

title: Ship Blocks the Suez Canal
desc: A giant container ship got stuck in the Suez Canal and blocked one of the world's busiest waterways for days.

title: Tourists Reach the Edge of Space
desc: Private companies flew paying passengers to the edge of space, opening a new era of space tourism.

title: Squid Game Becomes a Hit
desc: The South Korean series Squid Game became one of the most-watched shows in streaming history.

title: First Webb Telescope Images
desc: The James Webb Space Telescope shared its first dazzling images of distant galaxies and nebulae.

title: Russia Invades Ukraine
desc: Russia launched a large invasion of neighboring Ukraine, beginning a major war in Europe.

title: Argentina Wins the World Cup
desc: Argentina, led by Lionel Messi, won soccer's World Cup in Qatar.

title: Queen Elizabeth II Dies
desc: Queen Elizabeth II of the United Kingdom passed away after the longest reign in British history.

title: Beijing Winter Olympics
desc: Beijing hosted the Winter Olympic Games, becoming the first city to host both summer and winter games.

title: ChatGPT Released
desc: A chatbot called ChatGPT launched and showed how powerful and popular artificial intelligence could be.

title: Artemis I Flies to the Moon
desc: NASA's Artemis I mission sent an uncrewed capsule around the Moon to test for future astronaut trips.

title: Spacecraft Nudges an Asteroid
desc: NASA's DART mission crashed into a small asteroid on purpose to test how to protect Earth.

title: King Charles III Crowned
desc: King Charles III was crowned in a grand coronation ceremony in London.

title: Turkey and Syria Earthquake
desc: A powerful earthquake struck Turkey and Syria, and rescuers from many nations rushed to help.

title: India Lands on the Moon
desc: India's Chandrayaan-3 mission landed near the Moon's south pole, a first for any country.

title: Barbie and Oppenheimer
desc: Two very different films, Barbie and Oppenheimer, opened the same weekend and drew huge crowds.

title: India Most Populous Nation
desc: India passed China to become the most populous country in the world.

title: Spain Win the Women's World Cup
desc: Spain won soccer's Women's World Cup, held in Australia and New Zealand.

title: Paris Summer Olympics
desc: Paris hosted the Summer Olympic Games, with an opening ceremony held along the Seine River.

title: Eclipse Crosses North America
desc: A total solar eclipse traveled across Mexico, the United States, and Canada, thrilling millions of watchers.

title: Giant Rocket Booster Caught
desc: SpaceX caught the returning booster of its huge Starship rocket with mechanical arms for the first time.

title: Notre-Dame Reopens
desc: The restored Notre-Dame cathedral in Paris reopened to the public after years of careful rebuilding.

title: Spain Win Euro 2024
desc: Spain won the European soccer championship, held in Germany.

title: First Private Spacewalk
desc: The Polaris Dawn crew performed the first spacewalk done by private, non-government astronauts.
